key_insight: Likelihood-based deep neural networks can recover smooth regression functions under speckle noise at essentially the same minimax rate as standard Gaussian-noise regression, up to logarithmic factors.
engineering_relevance: This provides a theoretically justified path for despeckling in coherent imaging systems such as SAR, OCT, and digital holography when multiplicative noise breaks ordinary least-squares assumptions.
method: Model speckle plus additive Gaussian noise and fit the regression function with likelihood-based DNN estimators.
Derive finite-sample upper bounds for low-dimensional and sparse high-dimensional settings.
Prove matching minimax lower bounds, showing the rate is near-optimal up to logs.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports numerical experiments showing the proposed DNN despeckling methods are consistent and effective.
watch_item: The guarantees are asymptotic up to logarithmic factors and depend on likelihood modeling assumptions, so performance may degrade if the noise model is misspecified.
paper_title: Minimax Theory of Likelihood-Based Deep Learning for Speckle Regression
